questions.question: Bryophytes are non-vascular plants that require a moist environment to grow.
questions.explanation: Bryophytes need water for growth, which is why they live in moist places. This can be found on page 12 of the content.

questions.question: Xylem is a vascular tissue responsible for transporting ______ from the roots to the rest of the plant.
questions.explanation: Xylem transports water and minerals throughout the plant. This can be found on page 17 of the content.

questions.question: Xerophytes are plants that survive in dry conditions and have deep roots to reach underground ______.
questions.explanation: Xerophytes live in dry places, but they can still get water from their deep roots. This can be found on page 20 of the content.

questions.question: Stomata are small openings on the surface of leaves that help in ______.
questions.explanation: Stomata allow carbon dioxide to enter and release oxygen. This can be found on page 25 of the content.

questions.question: Monocots have ______ in their seeds, while dicots have two cotyledons.
questions.hint: Number of cotyledons in monocot and dicot seeds
questions.explanation: Monocots have one cotyledon, while dicots have two. This can be found on page 30 of the content.

questions.question: Angiosperms are plants that produce ______ in their flowers.
questions.explanation: Angiosperms produce seeds in their flowers. This can be found on page 35 of the content.

questions.question: Gymnosperms are plants that produce ______ in cones, not seeds in flowers like angiosperms.
questions.explanation: Gymnosperms produce seeds in their cones. This can be found on page 40 of the content.

questions.question: The root type that grows straight into the soil is a ______.
questions.explanation: Taproots grow straight down into the ground. This can be found on page 45 of the content.

questions.question: The part of a plant that carries water and minerals from the roots to the rest of the plant is called the ______.
questions.explanation: Stems transport water and minerals throughout the plant. This can be found on page 50 of the content.

questions.question: Leaves are responsible for ______ through a process called photosynthesis.
questions.explanation: Leaves produce food through photosynthesis. This can be found on page 55 of the content.

questions.question: The fruit of a plant is responsible for ______ and dispersal.
questions.explanation: Fruits carry seeds and help disperse them. This can be found on page 60 of the content.

questions.question: Photosynthesis occurs when light energy is converted into chemical energy through the process of ______.
questions.hint: Process of converting light energy to chemical energy
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis is how plants convert sunlight into food. This can be found on page 65 of the content.

questions.question: Roots absorb water and minerals from the soil through a process called ______.
questions.explanation: Roots get water and minerals by absorption. This can be found on page 70 of the content.

questions.question: Transpiration is the process of water being transported through a plant's ______ and released into the air as water vapor.
questions.hint: Process of water transportation in plants
questions.explanation: Transpiration occurs when water moves up through a plant's stomata. This can be found on page 75 of the content.

questions.question: Respiration is the process by which plants release carbon dioxide and oxygen through their ______.
questions.hint: Process of releasing gases in plants
questions.explanation: Plants respire through their stomata, releasing CO2 and O2. This can be found on page 80 of the content.
